title: PolicyDecisionSchema
description: The output of a single Policy Engine evaluation. Functions as a time-bound capability ticket: no ExecutionAdapter may act on an ActionProposal without a matching, unexpired, hash-valid PolicyDecision with decision == 'allow'.

policy_schema_version.description: Version of this schema (the shape of a PolicyDecision document).

risk_classifier_version.description: Which version of the Risk Classifier produced the risk_tier used in this decision.

input_hash.description: sha256 of the exact ActionProposal payload this decision was issued for. ExecutionAdapter must re-verify this before acting.

overridden.description: True if this rule's effect was overridden to 'allow' by a valid HILDecision.

required_approvals.description: Roles still required before this decision could become 'allow'. Empty once satisfied.

hil_decision_ids.description: HILDecisions consumed as input to this evaluation, if any.

expires_at.description: Hard expiry, always a concrete timestamp (deny_permanent uses a far-future sentinel rather than null, to keep 'is this ticket still valid' a single, uniform comparison everywhere downstream). On expiry, the Execution Adapter must refuse and the Policy Engine must be re-invoked to issue a fresh PolicyDecision — a HILDecision never extends an existing PolicyDecision's expiry, it only ever becomes input to a brand-new evaluation.

previous_decision_id.description: Links to the prior PolicyDecision for the same action_proposal_id, forming the logical decision chain (distinct from the audit hash-chain).

decision_hash.description: sha256 over the canonical serialization of this decision's own content (excluding decision_hash itself), for tamper-evidence.
